turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having a really bad cough for the past five days. It started out dry and now it's bringing up yellowish-green stuff, which is really worrying me because I'm an elementary school teacher and I work with young kids every day.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The cough. It's constant and gets worse at night. The productive part of it started about 4 days in, and it's really yellowish-green, like phlegm or mucus. I'm getting a bit worried because my students are always around me and I don't want to risk giving them anything if I can avoid it.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 5 days ago, with just a dry cough at first. It wasn't too bad, but then over the past few days, it's gotten worse and turned into this productive cough that's bringing up yellowish-green stuff.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all the time, really. I mean, every hour or so, I'm coughing up something, whether it's just a little bit of mucus or a bigger amount like yesterday evening when I was worried that I might have brought up some blood - but it turned out to be just more yellowish-green stuff.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 or 9. The dry cough was annoying, but once it turned productive and started bringing up that yucky green stuff, it got really uncomfortable and worrisome.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: When I'm coughing, I also get a bit short of breath and I can hear this wheezing sound in my chest. It's not constant, but it's definitely there when I'm coughing, especially if I've been exerting myself or laughing too hard with my partner.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It gets a lot worse when I exert myself, like when I'm teaching a class of energetic kids or trying to keep up with them on a playground outing. But even just talking can make it worse too, especially if I have to raise my voice. It's really exhausting and makes me feel short of breath.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing night sweats for the past 4 nights - they're really bad, to the point where I'm soaking through my pajamas every night. Other than that, no, I don't usually feel nauseous or dizzy when I'm coughing, but it does make me feel a bit lightheaded if I exert myself too much.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. It started just in my chest on one side, but when I cough, it feels like it radiates down into my back and sometimes even up into my throat. The sharp pain is mostly on the right side, which gets worse with deep breathing and coughing.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My legs and feet feel fine, no swelling at all. But to be honest, with this constant coughing and wheezing, I've been a bit too worried about my students getting sick to really notice if anything else is going on with me. It's just the cough that's been taking up most of my attention.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season, but I've also been using over-the-counter dextromethorphan for the past week because the cough was really bothering me.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not sure if I have anything like that. My doctor said everything was normal when I had my physical exam about a year ago, but I don't really know what to expect with this cough. Can you tell me more about why you're asking?

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure if I've ever had my cholesterol checked. To be honest, I don't really recall discussing that during my last physical exam with my doctor about a year ago. My main concern right now is this cough and making sure it doesn't affect my students at school.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. As for drinking, I might have one to two glasses of wine on special occasions like weekends when I'm with my partner, but that's about it. To be honest, I'm a bit more worried about making sure this cough doesn't affect my students than I am about drinking habits right now.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, with this cough going on, I haven't really been thinking about my diet or exercise habits. But I do work out occasionally - maybe 2-3 times a week, just light stuff like walking or yoga - and try to eat relatively healthy, lots of fruits and vegetables, whole grains... nothing too exciting. It's not like I've had time to think about it with this cough weighing on my mind all the time.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mother actually has asthma, and that's something she's been managing for a while now. I'm not sure if there's any direct connection to my current symptoms, but it's definitely something on my mind given our conversation so far.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mother has asthma, like I mentioned before. That's really about all I know of any serious medical conditions in my family. My mom's been managing it for a while now, so that's been on my mind a bit given our conversation today.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It was about a year ago. Everything checked out normal at that point, but I'm not sure what to expect with this cough. I've been taking care of myself as best I can, but it's been really worrying me, especially since I work with kids every day and don't want to risk getting them sick.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I'm allergic to penicillin. It causes a rash.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: To be honest, with my students always getting sick and me being around them all day, it's been on my mind that several of them have had upper respiratory infections in the past month. I've been trying to take precautions like washing my hands a lot and keeping surfaces clean, but it's still worrying me that this cough could be contagious.
